title: mesoneuron hallieri
intro: Mesoneuron hallieri, commonly known as the Hallier's Mesoneuron, is a plant species belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. The therapeutic actions of Mesoneuron hallieri include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which contribute to its use in alleviating pain and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Mesoneuron hallieri can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting the plant's leaves or roots can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, especially in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to skin irritation, rashes, and allergic reactions in some individuals. If not handled carefully, mesoneuron hallieri can also cause respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath.
